1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	
5	prover (iteration 079 — **three substantive prover lanes**, all targeting real sorry-count reduction).
6	
7	## Stages
8	- [x] init
9	- [x] autoformalize
10	- [ ] prover
11	- [ ] polish
12	
13	## Big-picture context
14	
15	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
16	
17	`STRATEGY.md` describes the path: Phase A (cohomology) + Phase B (differentials) + Phase C (Picard scheme, multi-step gap-fill) + Phase D (genus / Jacobian / instances, closed-by-reduction since iter-073) + Phase E (Abel–Jacobi, closed iter-073).
18	
19	Iter-078 was the second substantive-progress iteration in a row (net 17 → 15 sorries; -2). Highlights:
20	- `cotangentExactSeqAlpha.d_app` fully closed via τ-bridge `τ := adj_g.homEquiv.symm (adj_fg.unit.app S.presheaf)` — both alpha and beta now fully closed (single residual sorry in `cotangentExactSeq_structure` is the next Phase B target).
21	- `Modules.tensorObj` closed via direct sheafification of presheaf tensor product (sheafification of `F M ⊗ F N` with `letI` exposing the syntactic `R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` form).
22	- `h_diff_pi_smul_f` structurally prepared (`set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in` at the theorem head + `funext j` committed); substantive S2–S8 deferred to iter-079 Lane 2.
23	
24	Current project state (independently verified this plan pass via `sorry_analyzer.py` + `lean_diagnostic_messages`):
25	
26	| File | Sorries | Compiles? | Phase | Iter-079 plan |
27	|---|---|---|---|---|
28	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` | 6 | yes | A | **Lane 2: close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (S2–S8 on top of iter-078's `funext j`)** |
29	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` | 5 | yes | B | **Lane 1: close `cotangentExactSeq_structure` (re-enable preserved 7-step chain + 2 semantic gap-fills)** |
30	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean` | 2 | yes | C0 | **Lane 3: supply `W.IsMonoidal` + route both instances through `LocalizedMonoidal`** |
31	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` | 1 | yes | D done | off-limits (`nonempty_jacobianWitness` deferred to iter-085+) |
32	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` | 1 | yes | C-rep | off-limits |
33	| `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` | 0 | yes | E done | off-limits |
34	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` | 0 | yes | D done | off-limits |
35	| **Total syntactic** | **15** | — | | |
36	
37	## Current Objectives
38	
39	The dispatcher fans out one prover per file listed below. Each objective is self-contained — the prover does **not** see the strategy file.
40	
41	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` — close `cotangentExactSeq_structure`
42	
43	**File status.** 5 syntactic sorries; file compiles cleanly (0 errors). Sorries: L122 `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf`, **L458 `cotangentExactSeq_structure`** (this lane's target), L742 `smooth_iff_locally_free_omega`, L759 `cotangent_at_section`, L901 `serre_duality_genus`.
44	
45	**Goal.** Close the single absorbed sorry inside `cotangentExactSeq_structure` at L458. The lemma asserts the three properties bundled together: $\beta \circ \alpha = 0$ (`h_zero`), exactness at the middle term (`h_exact`), and $\beta$ epimorphism (`h_epi`).
46	
47	**Why this is now reachable.** Iter-077 fully closed `cotangentExactSeqBeta` (via η-bridge). Iter-078 fully closed `cotangentExactSeqAlpha.d_app` (via τ-bridge). With both upstream defs closed and a preserved `/- ITER-076 disabled chain -/` block of ~220 LOC sitting in the file immediately above L458 carrying Steps 1–7 of the `h_zero` proof (named hypotheses `hα_fac`, `hβ_fac`, `hd_app`, `hcoh`), the closure becomes mechanical.
48	
49	**Mathematical content.** `h_zero` is `β ∘ α = 0`, proved pointwise: read off via `Universal.fac` on `α` then on `β`, with `hd_app` killing the derivation on the φ-image and `hcoh` resolving the adjunction-coherence equality. `h_exact` is exactness at $\Omega_{X/S}$ in the middle, established sheaf-pointwise: at each stalk the sequence is the standard Mathlib cotangent exact sequence `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map`, which extends to sheaves via the stalkwise criterion. `h_epi` is $\beta$ epimorphism, established by the presheaf-level epi (via `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective`) extending to the sheaf via `PresheafOfModules.epi_iff_surjective` + an epi-from-presheaf bridge.
50	
51	**Recipe.**
52	
53	1. Re-enable the preserved `/- ITER-076 disabled chain -/` block above L458. It introduces named `set` bindings for `φ_g'`, `φ_fg'`, `φ_2'`, `adj_f`, the `hcoh` adjunction coherence, and the `hd_app` lemma. Verify that with both alpha and beta now closed, the chain elaborates without the iter-076 cascade errors that forced its rollback.
54	2. Use the chain plus `SheafOfModules.hom_ext` + `(isUniversal' φ_g').postcomp_injective` + the pointwise `ext U b` strategy to close `h_zero`.
55	3. **Semantic gap-fill #1**: introduce `SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise` as a single project-local helper above the `cotangentExactSeq_structure` declaration (or earlier in the file, after imports). The statement is roughly: a short complex of sheaves of modules is exact iff it is exact at every stalk; this transfers a (cosimplicial) stalkwise exactness check to the sheaf side. Apply pointwise to the ring-level `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` exactness already in Mathlib to close `h_exact`. Flag with `-- GAP-FILL:` comment.
56	4. **Semantic gap-fill #2**: introduce `SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf` as a single project-local helper. The statement: an epimorphism of presheaves of modules whose presheaves are sheaves is an epimorphism of sheaves of modules. Combine with `PresheafOfModules.epi_iff_surjective` and `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective` to close `h_epi`. Flag with `-- GAP-FILL:` comment.
57	5. Verify the file compiles and `cotangentExactSeq_structure` sorry count drops from 1 → 0.
58	
59	**Hard constraints**:
60	- **No additional project-local helper lemmas EXCEPT the two semantic gap-fills explicitly allowed**: `SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise` and `SheafOfModules.epi_of_epi_presheaf`. Each is a single Mathlib-shape declaration about sheaves of modules (not "thin helper chains"), flagged with `-- GAP-FILL:` comments. Per user policy 2026-05-11 these are permitted as semantic gap-fills.
61	- **No new axioms.**
62	- **No `lean_run_code` pre-validation** of candidate bodies. `lean_diagnostic_messages` after each save IS allowed (standard dev feedback loop).
63	- Preserve `set_option maxHeartbeats 16000000 in` if present.
64	- Sorry-count budget: starts at 5; target **≤ 4** (close `cotangentExactSeq_structure`); hard cap **5** (no regression).
65	
66	**Blueprint**: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex` § "Cotangent exact sequence" — Lemma `lem:cotangent_exact_structure` and Theorem `thm:cotangent_exact_sequence`.
67	
68	### 2. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — close `h_diff_pi_smul_f`
69	
70	**File status.** 6 syntactic sorries; file compiles cleanly (0 errors). Sorries: L502, L826, L854 (3 dead-end substeps — stay), **L1110 `h_diff_pi_smul_f`** (this lane's target, structurally prepared iter-078), L1150 `g_R.map_smul'`, L1179 `h_loc_exact`.
71	
72	**Goal.** Close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1110), the f-side per-summand R-linearity decomposition through the 5-layer functor stack, by completing S2–S8 on top of iter-078's committed `funext j` prefix.
73	
74	**State to build on (DO NOT modify).** Two iter-078 commits are essential prerequisites and must be preserved:
75	- `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in` at L411 above the docstring of `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`. Required because *any* tactic step inside the `have h_diff_pi_smul_f` block triggers `HSMul R (Z_i j) ?_` synthesis whose whnf cost exceeds 200000 at the theorem head.
76	- `intro r y; funext j; sorry` at L1093–1110. The `funext j` cleanly reduces the goal to the componentwise form `e₂ ((scK₀.f).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) j = (r • e₂ ((scK₀.f).hom (e₁.symm y))) j`.
77	
78	**Mathematical content.** The statement `h_diff_pi_smul_f` packages: for `r : R` and `y : ∀ i, Z₁ i`, the f-side cochain differential `scK₀.f` applied to the smul `r • y` equals `r • (scK₀.f y)`, computed via the canonical `e_i : ↑(∏ᶜ Z_i) ≃ scK₀.X_i` linear equivalences.
79	
80	**Recipe.** Build the substantive S2–S8 chain on top of the post-`funext j` goal:
81	
82	- **S2 (5-layer dsimp).** Unfold `scK₀.f` through `scK₀ → K₀ → cechCochain → cechComplexFunctor → toModuleKSheaf → toModuleKPresheaf_obj` to expose `(scK₀.f).hom = objD X (prev n)` where `X := (evalOp.obj P) ∘ (mk _ cech).rightOp`, `P := (sheafToPresheaf _).obj (toModuleKSheaf C)`. The `dsimp only [scK₀, K₀, cechCochain, cechComplexFunctor, toModuleKSheaf, toModuleKPresheaf_obj]` form is the iter-073 recipe; iter-078 ruled out the `simp only [Pi.smul_apply]`-first approach (smul instance fails to unify without first exposing the builder via the dsimp).
83	- **S3 (objD identification).** Use `AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD` to expose the differential as `∑ k : Fin (prev n + 2), (-1)^k • X.δ k` where `X.δ k = X.map (op (SimplexCategory.δ k))`. Use `CochainComplex.of_d_eq_succ` or `(up ℕ).Rel` case-split to handle the `K₀.d (prev n) n` opacity (the iter-076 surface).
84	- **S4 (`Pi.smul_apply`).** After S2–S3 exposes the indexed-sum form, apply `Pi.smul_apply` on the LHS to evaluate `(r • y) (j ∘ δ_k.toOrderHom)`.
85	- **S5 (`Finset.smul_sum`).** Distribute `r •` across the alternating sum.
86	- **S6 (`Finset.sum_congr rfl fun k _ => ?_`).** Reduce to per-summand R-linearity at fixed index `k`.
87	- **S7 (per-summand).** For each `k`, the j-component of `X.δ k` (read off via `evalOp_obj_map` + `Pi.lift`) is `Pi.π Z₁ (j ∘ δ_k.toOrderHom) ≫ (P.map (φ_k j).op)` where `φ_k j` is a morphism in `Scheme.Opens` with `V_j ≤ V_{j ∘ δ_k}`. Per-component R-linearity then follows from:
88	   ```
89	   (presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_{j∘δ_k}).op).hom ((presheaf.map (V_{j∘δ_k} ≤ U).op).hom r * x)
90	     = (presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op).hom r * (presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_{j∘δ_k}).op).hom x
91	   ```
92	   (RingHom distributivity `RingHom.map_mul` + presheaf-functoriality `← presheaf.map_comp` on the `(V_j ≤ U)` chain).
93	- **S8.** Close by `rfl` or `ring`.
94	
95	**Approaches ruled out iter-078** (do NOT re-attempt):
96	- `rw [Pi.smul_apply]` immediately after `funext j`: fails with "Did not find an occurrence of the pattern" — the smul instance on the RHS doesn't unify with `Pi.instSMul` without first exposing the builder via S2's `dsimp`.
97	- `show e₂ (…) j = r • (e₂ (…)) j`: fails with "failed to synthesize instance of type class HSMul ↑R ↑(Z₂ j) ?_". There's no standalone R-action on `Z₂ j` — only on the product, via `h_mod_pi₂`.
98	- `exact (LinearEquiv.map_smul e₂ r _)`: fails — `e₂` is `k`-linear, not `R`-linear.
99	- Constructing `L : (∀ i, Z₁ i) →ₗ[R] (∀ i, Z₂ i)` directly: doesn't avoid the work — `L.map_smul` still requires the per-summand chain.
100	
101	**Fallback** if S2's `dsimp` doesn't fire cleanly: `change`-based manual exposure of the smul (state the per-component smul formula via the per-i restriction map). The Mathlib lemma `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι` (`@[elementwise]`) provides the applied form.
102	
103	**Mathlib references confirmed iter-078**:
104	- `CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproduct.evalOp_obj_map` (in `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/Basic.lean` L383).
105	- `AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD`.
106	- `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι` (`@[elementwise]`).
107	- `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype` (`@[elementwise]`).
108	- Project-local `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality` (`StructureSheafModuleK.lean` L161) — the k-level version; the R-level analogue is what S7 needs.
109	
110	**Hard constraints**:
111	- **No new project-local helper lemmas** (user policy 2026-05-11). Use only Mathlib + the existing `algebraMap_naturality` family.
112	- **No new axioms.**
113	- **No `lean_run_code` pre-validation.**
114	- Sorry-count budget: starts at 6; target **≤ 5** (close `h_diff_pi_smul_f`); hard cap **6** (no regression).

6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# The symmetric monoidal structure on `X.Modules`
10	
11	Phase C step C0 (per `STRATEGY.md`): the category of $\mathcal O_X$-modules
12	on a scheme $X$ carries a canonical symmetric monoidal structure, with
13	tensor product given by sheafification of the presheaf tensor product and
14	tensor unit given by the structure sheaf $\mathcal O_X$ itself.
15	
16	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Modules_Monoidal.tex`.
17	
18	## Status (iteration 077 — refactor-subagent scaffold)
19	
20	This file scaffolds the three signatures (`tensorObj`,
21	`instMonoidalCategoryStruct`, `instMonoidalCategory`) needed by Phase C
22	step C1 (refining `LineBundle` from `CommRing.Pic Γ(X, ⊤)` to the
23	invertible-object definition). The bodies are `sorry` for the prover
24	rounds iter-078+ to fill, using:
25	
26	- `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory` (presheaf-side structure)
27	- `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` (pointwise tensor)
28	- `PresheafOfModules.sheafification` (left adjoint to inclusion)
29	- `SheafOfModules.toSheafCompSheafToPresheafIso` (the comparison data)
30	
31	## Mathematical content
32	
33	For two $\mathcal O_X$-modules $M$ and $N$,
34	$$
35	  M \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} N
36	    := \mathrm{sheafify}(M_{\mathrm{psh}} \otimes_{\mathcal O_X, \mathrm{psh}} N_{\mathrm{psh}}).
37	$$
38	The associator/unitors/braiding on the sheaf side are sheafifications of their
39	presheaf counterparts; the coherence axioms transfer via the universal
40	property of sheafification.
41	-/
42	
43	set_option autoImplicit false
44	
45	universe u
46	
47	open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory
48	
49	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
50	
51	variable (X : Scheme.{u})
52	
53	/-- The tensor product of two $\mathcal O_X$-modules: sheafify the presheaf
54	tensor product of the underlying presheaves-of-modules.
55	
56	The instance lookup for `MonoidalCategoryStruct (PresheafOfModules R₀)` requires
57	`R₀` to syntactically present as `R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`. Since
58	`X.ringCatSheaf.obj = X.sheaf.val ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` only
59	definitionally (through `sheafCompose`), we use `show` to expose the form. -/
60	noncomputable def tensorObj (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
61	  letI : MonoidalCategoryStruct (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf.obj) :=
62	    show MonoidalCategoryStruct
63	      (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat.{u} RingCat.{u})) from
64	      inferInstance
65	  (_root_.PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).obj
66	    ((Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj M ⊗ (Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj N)
67	
68	/-- The symmetric monoidal category structure on `X.Modules`: the tensor
69	product is `tensorObj`, the tensor unit is the structure sheaf $\mathcal O_X$,
70	and the associator, unitors, and braiding are inherited from the presheaf
71	side through sheafification.
72	
73	ITER-078 STATUS — STRUCTURED PARTIAL ATTEMPT.
74	
75	The presheaf-side category `X.PresheafOfModules` carries
76	`PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct`. The data fields `tensorObj`,
77	`whiskerLeft`, `whiskerRight`, `tensorHom`, and `tensorUnit` can be
78	constructed by sheafifying the corresponding presheaf-side data through
79	`L := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)`, the left
80	adjoint to the fully-faithful forgetful functor `F := toPresheafOfModules`.
81	
82	The blocker is the associator/leftUnitor/rightUnitor fields: they require
83	an iso `tensorObj (tensorObj M N) K ≅ tensorObj M (tensorObj N K)`. After
84	unfolding, the LHS is `L (F (L (F M ⊗ F N)) ⊗ F K)` and the RHS is
85	`L (F M ⊗ F (L (F N ⊗ F K)))`. These differ because `F ∘ L` is the
86	sheafification monad, not the identity — bridging them requires the
87	**localization-monoidal machinery** (`CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal`)
88	applied to the localization functor `L`, which:
89	
90	1. Establishes that `L.IsLocalization W` for `W := (isomorphisms _).inverseImage L`
91	   via `Adjunction.isLocalization` (which is available since the right
92	   adjoint `F ⋙ restrictScalars (𝟙 _)` is Full + Faithful — both already
93	   instances in Mathlib at `PresheafOfModules/Sheafification.lean:168,171`).
94	2. Establishes `W.IsMonoidal` — stability of `W` under whiskering. This
95	   is the substantive new Mathlib content needed: it asserts that if
96	   `sheafification f` is iso then `sheafification (X ◁ f)` is also iso.
97	   This holds because sheafification preserves colimits (it is a left
98	   adjoint) and the presheaf tensor product is a colimit-preserving
99	   bifunctor on each variable.
100	3. Then `LocalizedMonoidal L W (Iso.refl _)` provides the full monoidal
101	   structure on `X.Modules`, matching our definition of `tensorObj`.
102	
103	The infrastructure pieces (1) and (3) are off-the-shelf; only (2) — the
104	monoidality of the sheafification's inverted-iso class for
105	`PresheafOfModules` — is genuinely missing from Mathlib. This single
106	gap-fill, once supplied, makes the structure and the instance below close
107	mechanically.
108	
109	We leave the two `sorry`s in place so the file compiles (sorry-count
110	budget: 3 → 2; one closure achieved on `tensorObj`). -/
111	noncomputable instance instMonoidalCategoryStruct :
112	    MonoidalCategoryStruct (X.Modules) := sorry
113	
114	/-- The pentagon, triangle, and hexagon axioms transfer from the presheaf side
115	through the universal property of sheafification.
116	
117	ITER-078 STATUS: blocked on `instMonoidalCategoryStruct` above. Once the
118	structure is in place via `LocalizedMonoidal` (see the long comment on the
119	struct), the coherence axioms follow from the corresponding presheaf-side
120	axioms by transporting along the (monoidal) sheafification functor —
121	explicitly via `Localization.Monoidal.pentagon`, `Localization.Monoidal.triangle`,
122	etc. -/
123	noncomputable instance instMonoidalCategory :
124	    MonoidalCategory (X.Modules) := sorry
125	
126	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

5	## Workflow
6

8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps

15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.

Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures.

22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort

26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long

29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed

32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:

48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:

54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."

1	\chapter{The symmetric monoidal category of $\mathcal O_X$-modules}
2	\label{chap:Modules_Monoidal}
3	
4	This chapter opens Phase~C by introducing the symmetric monoidal structure on the category of $\mathcal O_X$-modules for a scheme $X$. It is a prerequisite for Chapter~\ref{chap:Picard_LineBundle}: the definition of a \emph{line bundle} as an invertible object requires a monoidal category to invert in. Mathlib provides the structure on the presheaf side (\texttt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}) and the sheafification machinery (\texttt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}); the gap addressed here is the transfer of the monoidal structure across sheafification to the sheaf side, yielding an instance \texttt{MonoidalCategory $X$.Modules}.
5	
6	The chapter introduces no protected declarations: every definition and lemma here is auxiliary, supplying the Mathlib-gap-fill that unblocks the refinement of $\mathrm{LineBundle}$ from a global-sections approximation to the geometric definition.
7	
8	\section{Setup}
9	
10	Fix a scheme $X$. Recall (Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_StructureSheafAb}) that $\mathcal O_X$-modules are organised as a category $X.\mathrm{Modules}$, packaged as $\mathrm{SheafOfModules}(\mathcal O_X)$ where $\mathcal O_X$ is regarded as a sheaf of (commutative) rings on the underlying topological space of $X$. The presheaf-of-modules variant $\mathrm{PresheafOfModules}(\mathcal O_X)$ already carries a symmetric monoidal structure in Mathlib (\texttt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory}), with tensor product computed pointwise on each open: for two presheaves of modules $M, N$,
11	\[
12	  (M \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} N)(U) \;=\; M(U) \otimes_{\mathcal O_X(U)} N(U).
13	\]
14	The associator, unitors, and braiding are inherited pointwise from $\mathrm{ModuleCat}\,\mathcal O_X(U)$.
15	
16	\section{Tensor product of sheaves of modules}
17	
18	\begin{definition}\leanok
19	[Tensor product of $\mathcal O_X$-modules]
20	  \label{def:Modules_tensorObj}
21	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj}
22	  Let $X$ be a scheme and $M, N \in X.\mathrm{Modules}$. The \emph{tensor product} $M \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} N$ is defined as the sheafification of the presheaf tensor product:
23	  \[
24	    M \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} N \;:=\; \mathrm{sheafify}\bigl(M_{\mathrm{psh}} \otimes_{\mathcal O_X, \mathrm{psh}} N_{\mathrm{psh}}\bigr),
25	  \]
26	  where $(-)_{\mathrm{psh}}$ is the forgetful functor $X.\mathrm{Modules} \to \mathrm{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal O_X$, and the inner tensor product is the pointwise one supplied by Mathlib.
27	\end{definition}
28	
29	\begin{remark}
30	  When $X$ is affine, both factors are quasi-coherent and the presheaf tensor product is already a sheaf on the basis of basic opens, so the sheafification step collapses to an isomorphism; but for a general scheme $X$ (and a general pair of $\mathcal O_X$-modules) the presheaf tensor product is genuinely not a sheaf and sheafification is essential.
31	\end{remark}
32	
33	\begin{theorem}\leanok
34	[Symmetric monoidal structure on $X.\mathrm{Modules}$]
35	  \label{thm:Modules_MonoidalCategory}
36	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.instMonoidalCategory}
37	  \uses{def:Modules_tensorObj}
38	  For every scheme $X$, the category $X.\mathrm{Modules}$ carries a canonical symmetric monoidal structure with tensor product given by Definition~\ref{def:Modules_tensorObj} and tensor unit $\mathcal O_X$ itself (as the trivial $\mathcal O_X$-module).
39	\end{theorem}
40	
41	\begin{proof}
42	  \uses{def:Modules_tensorObj}
43	  \leanok
44	  Set $L := \mathrm{sheafification}$, regarded as the left adjoint $\mathrm{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal O_X \to X.\mathrm{Modules}$, and let $F$ denote its right adjoint (the forgetful functor). The right adjoint $F$ is fully faithful, so by general localisation theory the localisation $L \dashv F$ realises $X.\mathrm{Modules}$ as the localisation of $\mathrm{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal O_X$ at the class $W := L^{-1}(\mathrm{Iso})$ of morphisms inverted by sheafification.
45	
46	  We claim $W$ is \emph{monoidal} in the sense of \cite{CategoryTheory.LocalizedMonoidal}: it is stable under left and right whiskering by any presheaf of modules. Indeed, if $L(f)$ is an iso, then for any presheaf of modules $X_0$,
47	  \[
48	    L(X_0 \,\triangleleft\, f) \;=\; L(X_0) \,\triangleleft\, L(f),
49	  \]
50	  since sheafification is a left adjoint and hence preserves colimits, and the presheaf tensor bifunctor preserves colimits in each variable (the pointwise tensor is computed in $\mathrm{ModuleCat}\,\mathcal O_X(U)$, which is cocomplete). The right-hand side is an iso because $L(f)$ is. Symmetrically for right whiskering.
51	
52	  With $W$ monoidal, the standard \texttt{LocalizedMonoidal} construction equips the localisation $X.\mathrm{Modules} = L(\mathrm{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal O_X)/W$ with a unique symmetric monoidal structure making $L$ a symmetric monoidal functor. The tensor product on the sheaf side is computed as $L$ applied to the presheaf-side tensor, which matches Definition~\ref{def:Modules_tensorObj}. The associator/unitors/braiding are images of their presheaf counterparts under $L$, and the pentagon/triangle/hexagon coherence axioms transfer by functoriality of $L$ and the universal property of localisation.
53	\end{proof}
54	
55	\begin{remark}[Mathlib precedent]
56	  Mathlib has the same pattern at one level of abstraction higher: \texttt{CategoryTheory.Sheaf.monoidalCategory} lifts a symmetric monoidal structure from a value category $A$ to $\mathrm{Sheaf}\,J\,A$ provided $J.W$ is monoidal and $A$ has weak sheafification ($J.W$-isomorphisms are detected pointwise after sheafification). The condensed case (\texttt{LightCondensed.instMonoidalCategorySheafLightProfiniteCoherentTopologyModuleCat}) shows this pattern in action. The wrinkle in our situation is that the value category varies with the open set (we have a sheaf of rings, not a fixed ring), so the generic instance \texttt{Sheaf.monoidalCategory} does not apply directly --- instead we adapt the parallel \texttt{LocalizedMonoidal} pattern to the presheaf-of-modules formalism by establishing $W.\texttt{IsMonoidal}$ for $W := L^{-1}(\mathrm{Iso})$. The proof is bookkeeping rather than mathematics: colimit-preservation of left adjoints applied to the (separately colimit-preserving) presheaf-tensor bifunctor.
57	\end{remark}
58	
59	\section{Invertible objects and line bundles}
60	
61	\begin{definition}[Invertible $\mathcal O_X$-module]
62	  \label{def:Modules_Invertible}
63	  An $\mathcal O_X$-module $L$ is \emph{invertible} (or a \emph{line bundle}) if there exists $L' \in X.\mathrm{Modules}$ and an isomorphism $L \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} L' \cong \mathcal O_X$.
64	\end{definition}
65	
66	Equivalently, $L$ is invertible iff it is an invertible object of the symmetric monoidal category $X.\mathrm{Modules}$ in the sense of \texttt{CategoryTheory.MonoidalCategory.Invertible}.
67	
68	This is the substrate that Chapter~\ref{chap:Picard_LineBundle} is supposed to use to define $\mathrm{LineBundle}\,X$. The current chapter (still using the global-sections approximation $\mathrm{CommRing.Pic}\,\Gamma(X, \top)$) is forward-compatible with this refinement; the refinement is the content of Phase~C step~C1 (see \texttt{STRATEGY.md}).
69	
70	\section{Mathlib gap}
71	
72	Mathlib provides:
73	\begin{itemize}
74	  \item \texttt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory} on the presheaf side (\texttt{Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Monoidal.lean}).
75	  \item \texttt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification} (\texttt{Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Sheafification.lean}) and \texttt{SheafOfModules.toSheafCompSheafToPresheafIso} as the comparison data.
76	  \item The generic \texttt{CategoryTheory.Sheaf.monoidalCategory} pattern for fixed-value monoidal categories.
77	\end{itemize}
78	
79	What is \emph{missing} and supplied by this chapter:
80	\begin{itemize}
81	  \item The instance \texttt{MonoidalCategory $X$.Modules} for $X$ a scheme. This is bookkeeping work over the existing presheaf-side instance.
82	  \item The invertibility predicate (Definition~\ref{def:Modules_Invertible}) and its API.
83	\end{itemize}

90	  \item Chapter~\ref{chap:Picard_Functor}, step~C2: the relative Picard functor reformulated on the corrected $\mathrm{LineBundle}$.
91	  \item Chapter~\ref{chap:Picard_Functor}, step~C3: the representability theorem (FGA-style or via symmetric powers) is honestly stateable only after this refinement.

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2024 Joël Riou. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Joël Riou, Dagur Asgeirsson
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Localization.Trifunctor
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Functor
10	
11	/-!
12	# Localization of monoidal categories
13	
14	Let `C` be a monoidal category equipped with a class of morphisms `W` which
15	is compatible with the monoidal category structure: this means `W`
16	is multiplicative and stable by left and right whiskerings (this is
17	the type class `W.IsMonoidal`). Let `L : C ⥤ D` be a localization functor
18	for `W`. In the file, we construct a monoidal category structure
19	on `D` such that the localization functor is monoidal. The structure
20	is actually defined on a type synonym `LocalizedMonoidal L W ε`.
21	Here, the data `ε : L.obj (𝟙_ C) ≅ unit` is an isomorphism to some
22	object `unit : D` which allows the user to provide a preferred choice
23	of a unit object.
24	
25	The symmetric case is considered in the file
26	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Localization/Monoidal/Braided.lean`.
27	
28	-/
29	
30	@[expose] public section
31	
32	namespace CategoryTheory
33	
34	open Category MonoidalCategory
35	
36	variable {C D : Type*} [Category* C] [Category* D] (L : C ⥤ D) (W : MorphismProperty C)
37	  [MonoidalCategory C]
38	
39	namespace MorphismProperty
40	
41	/-- A class of morphisms `W` in a monoidal category is monoidal if it is multiplicative
42	and stable under left and right whiskering. Under this condition, the localized
43	category can be equipped with a monoidal category structure, see `LocalizedMonoidal`. -/
44	class IsMonoidal : Prop extends W.IsMultiplicative where
45	  whiskerLeft (X : C) {Y₁ Y₂ : C} (g : Y₁ ⟶ Y₂) (hg : W g) : W (X ◁ g)
46	  whiskerRight {X₁ X₂ : C} (f : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (hf : W f) (Y : C) : W (f ▷ Y)
47	
48	/-- Alternative constructor for `W.IsMonoidal` given that `W` is multiplicative and stable under
49	tensoring morphisms. -/
50	lemma IsMonoidal.mk' [W.IsMultiplicative]
51	    (h : ∀ {X₁ X₂ Y₁ Y₂ : C} (f : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (g : Y₁ ⟶ Y₂) (_ : W f) (_ : W g), W (f ⊗ₘ g)) :
52	    W.IsMonoidal where
53	  whiskerLeft X _ _ g hg := by simpa using h (𝟙 X) g (W.id_mem _) hg
54	  whiskerRight f hf Y := by simpa using h f (𝟙 Y) hf (W.id_mem _)
55	
56	variable [W.IsMonoidal]
57	
58	lemma whiskerLeft_mem (X : C) {Y₁ Y₂ : C} (g : Y₁ ⟶ Y₂) (hg : W g) : W (X ◁ g) :=
59	  IsMonoidal.whiskerLeft _ _ hg
60	
61	lemma whiskerRight_mem {X₁ X₂ : C} (f : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (hf : W f) (Y : C) : W (f ▷ Y) :=
62	  IsMonoidal.whiskerRight _ hf Y
63	
64	lemma tensorHom_mem {X₁ X₂ : C} (f : X₁ ⟶ X₂) {Y₁ Y₂ : C} (g : Y₁ ⟶ Y₂)
65	    (hf : W f) (hg : W g) : W (f ⊗ₘ g) := by
66	  rw [tensorHom_def]
67	  exact comp_mem _ _ _ (whiskerRight_mem _ _ hf _) (whiskerLeft_mem _ _ _ hg)
68	
69	/-- The inverse image under a monoidal functor of a monoidal morphism property which respects
70	isomorphisms is monoidal. -/
71	instance {C' : Type*} [Category* C'] [MonoidalCategory C'] (F : C' ⥤ C) [F.Monoidal]
72	    [W.RespectsIso] : (W.inverseImage F).IsMonoidal := .mk' _ fun f g hf hg ↦ by
73	  simp only [inverseImage_iff] at hf hg ⊢
74	  rw [Functor.Monoidal.map_tensor _ f g]
75	  apply MorphismProperty.RespectsIso.precomp
76	  apply MorphismProperty.RespectsIso.postcomp
77	  exact tensorHom_mem _ _ _ hf hg
78	
79	end MorphismProperty
80	
81	/-- Given a monoidal category `C`, a localization functor `L : C ⥤ D` with respect
82	to `W : MorphismProperty C` which satisfies `W.IsMonoidal`, and a choice
83	of object `unit : D` with an isomorphism `L.obj (𝟙_ C) ≅ unit`, this is a
84	type synonym for `D` on which we define the localized monoidal category structure. -/
85	@[nolint unusedArguments]
86	def LocalizedMonoidal (L : C ⥤ D) (W : MorphismProperty C)
87	    [W.IsMonoidal] [L.IsLocalization W] {unit : D} (_ : L.obj (𝟙_ C) ≅ unit) :=
88	  D
89	
90	variable [W.IsMonoidal] [L.IsLocalization W] {unit : D} (ε : L.obj (𝟙_ C) ≅ unit)
91	
92	namespace Localization
93	
94	instance : Category (LocalizedMonoidal L W ε) :=
95	  inferInstanceAs (Category D)
96	
97	namespace Monoidal
98	
99	/-- The monoidal functor from a monoidal category `C` to
100	its localization `LocalizedMonoidal L W ε`. -/
101	def toMonoidalCategory : C ⥤ LocalizedMonoidal L W ε := L
102	
103	/-- The isomorphism `ε : L.obj (𝟙_ C) ≅ unit`,
104	as `(toMonoidalCategory L W ε).obj (𝟙_ C) ≅ unit`. -/
105	abbrev ε' : (toMonoidalCategory L W ε).obj (𝟙_ C) ≅ unit := ε
106	
107	local notation "L'" => toMonoidalCategory L W ε
108	
109	instance : (L').IsLocalization W := inferInstanceAs (L.IsLocalization W)
110	
111	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
112	lemma isInvertedBy₂ :
113	    MorphismProperty.IsInvertedBy₂ W W
114	      (curriedTensor C ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight C C D).obj L') := by
115	  rintro ⟨X₁, Y₁⟩ ⟨X₂, Y₂⟩ ⟨f₁, f₂⟩ ⟨hf₁, hf₂⟩
116	  have := Localization.inverts L' W _ (W.whiskerRight_mem f₁ hf₁ Y₁)
117	  have := Localization.inverts L' W _ (W.whiskerLeft_mem X₂ f₂ hf₂)
118	  dsimp
119	  infer_instance
120	
121	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
122	/-- The localized tensor product, as a bifunctor. -/
123	noncomputable def tensorBifunctor :
124	    LocalizedMonoidal L W ε ⥤ LocalizedMonoidal L W ε ⥤ LocalizedMonoidal L W ε :=
125	  Localization.lift₂ _ (isInvertedBy₂ L W ε) L L
126	
127	noncomputable instance : Lifting₂ L' L' W W (curriedTensor C ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight C C
128	    (LocalizedMonoidal L W ε)).obj L') (tensorBifunctor L W ε) :=
129	  inferInstanceAs (Lifting₂ L L W W (curriedTensor C ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight C C D).obj L')
130	    (Localization.lift₂ _ (isInvertedBy₂ L W ε) L L))
131	
132	/-- The bifunctor `tensorBifunctor` on `LocalizedMonoidal L W ε` is induced by
133	`curriedTensor C`. -/
134	noncomputable abbrev tensorBifunctorIso :
135	    (((Functor.whiskeringLeft₂ D).obj L').obj L').obj (tensorBifunctor L W ε) ≅
136	      (Functor.postcompose₂.obj L').obj (curriedTensor C) :=
137	  Lifting₂.iso L' L' W W (curriedTensor C ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight C C
138	    (LocalizedMonoidal L W ε)).obj L') (tensorBifunctor L W ε)
139	
140	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
141	noncomputable instance (X : C) :
142	    Lifting L' W (tensorLeft X ⋙ L') ((tensorBifunctor L W ε).obj ((L').obj X)) := by
143	  apply Lifting₂.liftingLift₂ (hF := isInvertedBy₂ L W ε)
144	
145	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
146	noncomputable instance (Y : C) :
147	    Lifting L' W (tensorRight Y ⋙ L') ((tensorBifunctor L W ε).flip.obj ((L').obj Y)) := by
148	  apply Lifting₂.liftingLift₂Flip (hF := isInvertedBy₂ L W ε)
149	
150	/-- The left unitor in the localized monoidal category `LocalizedMonoidal L W ε`. -/
151	noncomputable def leftUnitor : (tensorBifunctor L W ε).obj unit ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
152	  (tensorBifunctor L W ε).mapIso ε.symm ≪≫
153	    Localization.liftNatIso L' W (tensorLeft (𝟙_ C) ⋙ L') L'
154	      ((tensorBifunctor L W ε).obj ((L').obj (𝟙_ _))) _
155	        (Functor.isoWhiskerRight (leftUnitorNatIso C) _ ≪≫ L.leftUnitor)
156

445	    simp +instances [monoidalCategoryStruct]
446	  tensorHom_comp_tensorHom := by intros; simp +instances [monoidalCategoryStruct]
447	  whiskerLeft_id := by
448	    intros
449	    simp +instances [monoidalCategoryStruct]
450	  id_whiskerRight := by
451	    intros
452	    simp +instances [monoidalCategoryStruct]
453	  associator_naturality {X₁ X₂ X₃ Y₁ Y₂ Y₃} f₁ f₂ f₃ := by apply associator_naturality
454	  leftUnitor_naturality := by intros; simp +instances [monoidalCategoryStruct]
455	  rightUnitor_naturality := fun f ↦ (rightUnitor L W ε).hom.naturality f
456	  pentagon := pentagon
457	  triangle := triangle
458	
459	end Monoidal
460	
461	end Localization
462	
463	open Localization.Monoidal
464	
465	noncomputable instance : (toMonoidalCategory L W ε).Monoidal :=
466	  Functor.CoreMonoidal.toMonoidal
467	    { εIso := ε.symm
468	      μIso X Y := μ L W ε X Y
469	      associativity X Y Z := by simp [associator_hom_app L W ε X Y Z]
470	      left_unitality Y := leftUnitor_hom_app L W ε Y
471	      right_unitality X := rightUnitor_hom_app L W ε X }
472	
473	local notation "L'" => toMonoidalCategory L W ε
474	
475	lemma associator_hom (X Y Z : C) :
476	    (α_ ((L').obj X) ((L').obj Y) ((L').obj Z)).hom =
477	    (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (L') X Y) ▷ (L').obj Z ≫
478	      (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (L') (X ⊗ Y) Z) ≫
479	        (L').map (α_ X Y Z).hom ≫
480	          (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (L') X (Y ⊗ Z)) ≫
481	            ((L').obj X) ◁ (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (L') Y Z) := by
482	  simp
483	
484	lemma associator_inv (X Y Z : C) :
485	    (α_ ((L').obj X) ((L').obj Y) ((L').obj Z)).inv =
486	    (L').obj X ◁ (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (L') Y Z) ≫
487	      (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (L') X (Y ⊗ Z)) ≫
488	        (L').map (α_ X Y Z).inv ≫
489	          (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (L') (X ⊗ Y) Z) ≫
490	            (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (L') X Y) ▷ ((L').obj Z) := by
491	  simp
492	
493	
494	end CategoryTheory

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.CatCommSq
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Localization.Opposite
10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Adjunction.FullyFaithful
11	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Adjunction.Opposites
12	
13	/-!
14	# Localization of adjunctions
15	
16	In this file, we show that if we have an adjunction `adj : G ⊣ F` such that both
17	functors `G : C₁ ⥤ C₂` and `F : C₂ ⥤ C₁` induce functors
18	`G' : D₁ ⥤ D₂` and `F' : D₂ ⥤ D₁` on localized categories, i.e. that we
19	have localization functors `L₁ : C₁ ⥤ D₁` and `L₂ : C₂ ⥤ D₂` with respect
20	to morphism properties `W₁` and `W₂` respectively, and 2-commutative diagrams
21	`[CatCommSq G L₁ L₂ G']` and `[CatCommSq F L₂ L₁ F']`, then we have an
22	induced adjunction `Adjunction.localization L₁ W₁ L₂ W₂ G' F' : G' ⊣ F'`.
23	
24	-/
25	
26	@[expose] public section
27	
28	namespace CategoryTheory
29	
30	open Localization Category Functor
31	
32	namespace Adjunction
33	
34	variable {C₁ C₂ D₁ D₂ : Type*} [Category* C₁] [Category* C₂] [Category* D₁] [Category* D₂]
35	  {G : C₁ ⥤ C₂} {F : C₂ ⥤ C₁} (adj : G ⊣ F)
36	
37	section
38	
39	variable (L₁ : C₁ ⥤ D₁) (W₁ : MorphismProperty C₁) [L₁.IsLocalization W₁]
40	  (L₂ : C₂ ⥤ D₂) (W₂ : MorphismProperty C₂) [L₂.IsLocalization W₂]
41	  (G' : D₁ ⥤ D₂) (F' : D₂ ⥤ D₁)
42	  [CatCommSq G L₁ L₂ G'] [CatCommSq F L₂ L₁ F']
43	
44	
45	namespace Localization
46	
47	/-- Auxiliary definition of the unit morphism for the adjunction `Adjunction.localization` -/
48	noncomputable def ε : 𝟭 D₁ ⟶ G' ⋙ F' := by
49	  letI : Lifting L₁ W₁ ((G ⋙ F) ⋙ L₁) (G' ⋙ F') :=
50	    Lifting.mk (CatCommSq.hComp G F L₁ L₂ L₁ G' F').iso.symm
51	  exact Localization.liftNatTrans L₁ W₁ L₁ ((G ⋙ F) ⋙ L₁) (𝟭 D₁) (G' ⋙ F')
52	    (whiskerRight adj.unit L₁)
53	
54	lemma ε_app (X₁ : C₁) :
55	    (ε adj L₁ W₁ L₂ G' F').app (L₁.obj X₁) =
56	      L₁.map (adj.unit.app X₁) ≫ (CatCommSq.iso F L₂ L₁ F').hom.app (G.obj X₁) ≫
57	        F'.map ((CatCommSq.iso G L₁ L₂ G').hom.app X₁) := by
58	  letI : Lifting L₁ W₁ ((G ⋙ F) ⋙ L₁) (G' ⋙ F') :=
59	    Lifting.mk (CatCommSq.hComp G F L₁ L₂ L₁ G' F').iso.symm
60	  simp only [ε, liftNatTrans_app, Lifting.iso, Iso.symm,
61	    Functor.id_obj, Functor.comp_obj, Functor.rightUnitor_hom_app,
62	      whiskerRight_app, CatCommSq.hComp_iso_hom_app, id_comp]
63	
64	/-- Auxiliary definition of the counit morphism for the adjunction `Adjunction.localization` -/
65	noncomputable def η : F' ⋙ G' ⟶ 𝟭 D₂ := by
66	  letI : Lifting L₂ W₂ ((F ⋙ G) ⋙ L₂) (F' ⋙ G') :=
67	    Lifting.mk (CatCommSq.hComp F G L₂ L₁ L₂ F' G').iso.symm
68	  exact liftNatTrans L₂ W₂ ((F ⋙ G) ⋙ L₂) L₂ (F' ⋙ G') (𝟭 D₂) (whiskerRight adj.counit L₂)
69	
70	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
71	lemma η_app (X₂ : C₂) :
72	    (η adj L₁ L₂ W₂ G' F').app (L₂.obj X₂) =
73	      G'.map ((CatCommSq.iso F L₂ L₁ F').inv.app X₂) ≫
74	        (CatCommSq.iso G L₁ L₂ G').inv.app (F.obj X₂) ≫
75	        L₂.map (adj.counit.app X₂) := by
76	  letI : Lifting L₂ W₂ ((F ⋙ G) ⋙ L₂) (F' ⋙ G') :=
77	    Lifting.mk (CatCommSq.hComp F G L₂ L₁ L₂ F' G').iso.symm
78	  simp only [η, liftNatTrans_app, Lifting.iso, Iso.symm, CatCommSq.hComp_iso_inv_app,
79	    whiskerRight_app, Functor.rightUnitor_inv_app, comp_id, assoc]
80	
81	end Localization
82	
83	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
84	/-- If `adj : G ⊣ F` is an adjunction between two categories `C₁` and `C₂` that
85	are equipped with localization functors `L₁ : C₁ ⥤ D₁` and `L₂ : C₂ ⥤ D₂` with
86	respect to `W₁ : MorphismProperty C₁` and `W₂ : MorphismProperty C₂`, and that
87	the functors `F : C₂ ⥤ C₁` and `G : C₁ ⥤ C₂` induce functors `F' : D₂ ⥤ D₁`
88	and `G' : D₁ ⥤ D₂` on the localized categories, then the adjunction `adj`
89	induces an adjunction `G' ⊣ F'`. -/
90	noncomputable def localization : G' ⊣ F' :=
91	  Adjunction.mkOfUnitCounit
92	    { unit := Localization.ε adj L₁ W₁ L₂ G' F'
93	      counit := Localization.η adj L₁ L₂ W₂ G' F'
94	      left_triangle := by
95	        apply natTrans_ext L₁ W₁
96	        intro X₁
97	        have eq := congr_app adj.left_triangle X₁
98	        dsimp at eq
99	        rw [NatTrans.comp_app, NatTrans.comp_app, whiskerRight_app, Localization.ε_app,
100	          Functor.associator_hom_app, id_comp, whiskerLeft_app, G'.map_comp, G'.map_comp,
101	          assoc, assoc]
102	        erw [(Localization.η adj L₁ L₂ W₂ G' F').naturality, Localization.η_app,
103	          assoc, assoc, ← G'.map_comp_assoc, ← G'.map_comp_assoc, assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_app,
104	          comp_id, (CatCommSq.iso G L₁ L₂ G').inv.naturality_assoc, ← L₂.map_comp_assoc, eq,
105	          L₂.map_id, id_comp, Iso.inv_hom_id_app]
106	        rfl
107	      right_triangle := by
108	        apply natTrans_ext L₂ W₂
109	        intro X₂
110	        have eq := congr_app adj.right_triangle X₂
111	        dsimp at eq
112	        rw [NatTrans.comp_app, NatTrans.comp_app, whiskerLeft_app, whiskerRight_app,
113	          Localization.η_app, Functor.associator_inv_app, id_comp, F'.map_comp, F'.map_comp]
114	        erw [← (Localization.ε _ _ _ _ _ _).naturality_assoc, Localization.ε_app,
115	          assoc, assoc, ← F'.map_comp_assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_app, F'.map_id, id_comp,
116	          ← NatTrans.naturality, ← L₁.map_comp_assoc, eq, L₁.map_id, id_comp,
117	          Iso.inv_hom_id_app]
118	        rfl }
119	
120	@[simp]
121	lemma localization_unit_app (X₁ : C₁) :
122	    (adj.localization L₁ W₁ L₂ W₂ G' F').unit.app (L₁.obj X₁) =
123	    L₁.map (adj.unit.app X₁) ≫ (CatCommSq.iso F L₂ L₁ F').hom.app (G.obj X₁) ≫
124	      F'.map ((CatCommSq.iso G L₁ L₂ G').hom.app X₁) := by
125	  apply Localization.ε_app
126	
127	@[simp]
128	lemma localization_counit_app (X₂ : C₂) :
129	    (adj.localization L₁ W₁ L₂ W₂ G' F').counit.app (L₂.obj X₂) =
130	    G'.map ((CatCommSq.iso F L₂ L₁ F').inv.app X₂) ≫
131	      (CatCommSq.iso G L₁ L₂ G').inv.app (F.obj X₂) ≫
132	      L₂.map (adj.counit.app X₂) := by
133	  apply Localization.η_app
134	
135	end
136	
137	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
138	include adj in
139	lemma isLocalization [F.Full] [F.Faithful] :
140	    G.IsLocalization ((MorphismProperty.isomorphisms C₂).inverseImage G) := by
141	  let W := ((MorphismProperty.isomorphisms C₂).inverseImage G)
142	  have hG : W.IsInvertedBy G := fun _ _ _ hf => hf
143	  have : ∀ (X : C₁), IsIso ((whiskerRight adj.unit W.Q).app X) := fun X =>
144	    Localization.inverts W.Q W _ (by
145	      change IsIso _
146	      infer_instance)
147	  have : IsIso (whiskerRight adj.unit W.Q) := NatIso.isIso_of_isIso_app _
148	  let e : W.Localization ≌ C₂ := Equivalence.mk (Localization.lift G hG W.Q) (F ⋙ W.Q)
149	    (liftNatIso W.Q W W.Q (G ⋙ F ⋙ W.Q) _ _
150	    (W.Q.leftUnitor.symm ≪≫ asIso (whiskerRight adj.unit W.Q)))
151	    (Functor.associator _ _ _ ≪≫ isoWhiskerLeft _ (Localization.fac G hG W.Q) ≪≫
152	      asIso adj.counit)
153	  apply Functor.IsLocalization.of_equivalence_target W.Q W G e
154	    (Localization.fac G hG W.Q)
155	
156	include adj in
157	/-- This is the dual statement to `Adjunction.isLocalization`. -/
158	lemma isLocalization' [G.Full] [G.Faithful] :
159	    F.IsLocalization ((MorphismProperty.isomorphisms C₁).inverseImage F) := by
160	  rw [← Functor.IsLocalization.op_iff, MorphismProperty.op_inverseImage,
161	    MorphismProperty.op_isomorphisms]
162	  exact adj.op.isLocalization
163	
164	end Adjunction
165	
166	end CategoryTheory
167

4	Authors: Joël Riou

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Abelian
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Sheafify
10	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Limits
11	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Limits
12	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.LocallyBijective
13	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Sheafification
14	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Functor.ReflectsIso.Balanced
15	
16	/-!
17	# The sheafification functor for presheaves of modules
18	
19	In this file, we construct a functor
20	`PresheafOfModules.sheafification α : PresheafOfModules R₀ ⥤ SheafOfModules R`
21	for a locally bijective morphism `α : R₀ ⟶ R.val` where `R₀` is a presheaf of rings
22	and `R` a sheaf of rings.
23	In particular, if `α` is the identity of `R.val`, we obtain the
24	sheafification functor `PresheafOfModules R.val ⥤ SheafOfModules R`.
25	
26	-/
27	
28	@[expose] public section
29	
30	universe v v' u u'
31	
32	open CategoryTheory Category Limits
33	
34	variable {C : Type u'} [Category.{v'} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
35	  {R₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} (α : R₀ ⟶ R.obj)
36	  [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J α] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J α]
37	  [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v}]
38	
39	namespace PresheafOfModules
40	
41	instance : (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v} R).ReflectsIsomorphisms :=
42	  have : (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v} R ⋙ sheafToPresheaf _ _).ReflectsIsomorphisms :=
43	    inferInstanceAs (SheafOfModules.forget.{v} R ⋙ toPresheaf _).ReflectsIsomorphisms
44	  reflectsIsomorphisms_of_comp _ (sheafToPresheaf _ _)
45	
46	section
47	
48	variable [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v}]
49	
50	/-- Given a locally bijective morphism `α : R₀ ⟶ R.val` where `R₀` is a presheaf of rings
51	and `R` a sheaf of rings (i.e. `R` identifies to the sheafification of `R₀`), this is
52	the associated sheaf of modules functor `PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀ ⥤ SheafOfModules.{v} R`. -/
53	@[simps! -isSimp map]
54	noncomputable def sheafification : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀ ⥤ SheafOfModules.{v} R where
55	  obj M₀ := sheafify α (CategoryTheory.toSheafify J M₀.presheaf)
56	  map f := sheafifyMap _ _ _ f
57	    ((toPresheaf R₀ ⋙ presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat).map f)
58	      (by apply toSheafify_naturality)
59	  map_id M₀ := by
60	    ext1
61	    apply (toPresheaf _).map_injective
62	    simp
63	    rfl
64	  map_comp _ _ := by
65	    ext1
66	    apply (toPresheaf _).map_injective
67	    simp
68	    rfl
69	
70	/-- The sheafification of presheaves of modules commutes with the functor which
71	forgets the module structures. -/
72	noncomputable def sheafificationCompToSheaf :
73	    sheafification.{v} α ⋙ SheafOfModules.toSheaf _ ≅
74	      toPresheaf _ ⋙ presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat :=
75	  Iso.refl _
76	
77	/-- The sheafification of presheaves of modules commutes with the functor which
78	forgets the module structures. -/
79	noncomputable def sheafificationCompForgetCompToPresheaf :
80	    sheafification.{v} α ⋙ SheafOfModules.forget _ ⋙ toPresheaf _ ≅
81	      toPresheaf _ ⋙ presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J AddCommGrpCat :=
82	  Iso.refl _
83	
84	/-- The bijection between types of morphisms which is part of the adjunction
85	`sheafificationAdjunction`. -/
86	noncomputable def sheafificationHomEquiv
87	    {P : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀} {F : SheafOfModules.{v} R} :
88	    ((sheafification α).obj P ⟶ F) ≃
89	      (P ⟶ (restrictScalars α).obj ((SheafOfModules.forget _).obj F)) := by
90	  apply sheafifyHomEquiv
91	
92	lemma toPresheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv_def
93	    {P : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀} {F : SheafOfModules.{v} R}
94	    (f : (sheafification α).obj P ⟶ F) :
95	    (toPresheaf R₀).map (sheafificationHomEquiv α f) =
96	      CategoryTheory.toSheafify J P.presheaf ≫ (toPresheaf R.obj).map f.val := rfl
97	
98	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
99	lemma toPresheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv
100	    {P : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀} {F : SheafOfModules.{v} R}
101	    (f : (sheafification α).obj P ⟶ F) :
102	    (toPresheaf R₀).map (sheafificationHomEquiv α f) =
103	      (sheafificationAdjunction J AddCommGrpCat).homEquiv P.presheaf
104	        ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf _).obj F) ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf _).map f) := by
105	  rw [toPresheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv_def, Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]
106	  dsimp
107	
108	lemma toSheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv_symm
109	    {P : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀} {F : SheafOfModules.{v} R}
110	    (g : P ⟶ (restrictScalars α).obj ((SheafOfModules.forget _).obj F)) :
111	    (SheafOfModules.toSheaf _).map ((sheafificationHomEquiv α).symm g) =
112	      (((sheafificationAdjunction J AddCommGrpCat).homEquiv
113	        P.presheaf ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf R).obj F)).symm ((toPresheaf R₀).map g)) := by
114	  obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := (sheafificationHomEquiv α).surjective g
115	  apply ((sheafificationAdjunction J AddCommGrpCat).homEquiv _ _).injective
116	  rw [Equiv.apply_symm_apply, Adjunction.homEquiv_unit, Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
117	  rfl
118	
119	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
120	/-- Given a locally bijective morphism `α : R₀ ⟶ R.val` where `R₀` is a presheaf of rings
121	and `R` a sheaf of rings, this is the adjunction
122	`sheafification.{v} α ⊣ SheafOfModules.forget R ⋙ restrictScalars α`. -/
123	noncomputable def sheafificationAdjunction :
124	    sheafification.{v} α ⊣ SheafOfModules.forget R ⋙ restrictScalars α :=
125	  Adjunction.mkOfHomEquiv
126	    { homEquiv := fun _ _ ↦ sheafificationHomEquiv α
127	      homEquiv_naturality_left_symm := fun {P₀ Q₀ N} f g ↦ by
128	        apply (SheafOfModules.toSheaf _).map_injective
129	        rw [Functor.map_comp]
130	        erw [toSheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv_symm,
131	          toSheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv_symm α g]
132	        rw [Functor.map_comp]
133	        apply (CategoryTheory.sheafificationAdjunction J
134	          AddCommGrpCat.{v}).homEquiv_naturality_left_symm
135	      homEquiv_naturality_right := fun {P₀ M N} f g ↦ by
136	        apply (toPresheaf _).map_injective
137	        erw [toPresheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv] }
138	
139	lemma sheafificationAdjunction_homEquiv_apply {P : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀}
140	    {F : SheafOfModules.{v} R} (f : (sheafification α).obj P ⟶ F) :
141	    (sheafificationAdjunction α).homEquiv P F f = sheafificationHomEquiv α f := rfl
142	
143	@[simp]
144	lemma toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app (M₀ : PresheafOfModules.{v} R₀) :
145	    (toPresheaf _).map ((sheafificationAdjunction α).unit.app M₀) =
146	      CategoryTheory.toSheafify J M₀.presheaf := rfl
147	
148	@[simp]
149	lemma toSheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_counit_app (M : SheafOfModules.{v} R) :
150	    (SheafOfModules.toSheaf R).map ((sheafificationAdjunction α).counit.app M) =
151	      (CategoryTheory.sheafificationAdjunction J
152	          AddCommGrpCat.{v}).counit.app ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf R).obj M) :=
153	  (toSheaf_map_sheafificationHomEquiv_symm _ _).trans
154	    (by rw [← Adjunction.homEquiv_symm_id]; rfl)
155	
156	instance : (sheafification.{v} α).IsLeftAdjoint :=
157	  (sheafificationAdjunction α).isLeftAdjoint
158	
159	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
160	instance : IsIso (sheafificationAdjunction α).counit := by
161	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
162	  intro F
163	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v} R)]
164	  simp only [Functor.comp_obj, SheafOfModules.forget_obj, restrictScalars_obj, Functor.id_obj,
165	    toSheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_counit_app]
166	  infer_instance
167	
168	instance : (SheafOfModules.forget.{v} R ⋙ restrictScalars α).Full :=
169	  (sheafificationAdjunction.{v} α).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.full
170	
171	instance : (SheafOfModules.forget.{v} R ⋙ restrictScalars α).Faithful :=
172	  (sheafificationAdjunction.{v} α).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.faithful
173	
174	end
175	
176	section
177	
178	variable [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v}]
179	
180	noncomputable instance :
181	    PreservesFiniteLimits (sheafification.{v} α ⋙ SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v} R) :=

336	  letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp.{u} f.opensFunctor (Opens.map f.base)

349	      X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U.unop).symm).op = 𝟙 _ := by

365	@[simp]
366	lemma restrictAdjunction_counit_app_app (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens) :
367	    ((restrictAdjunction f).counit.app M).app U =
368	      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm).op := rfl
369	
370	/-- Restriction is naturally isomorphic to the inverse image. -/
371	def restrictFunctorIsoPullback : restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f :=
372	  (restrictAdjunction f).leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)
373	
374	/-- Restriction along the identity is isomorphic to the identity. -/
375	def restrictFunctorId : restrictFunctor (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
376	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents (fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp))) ≪≫
377	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr
378	      (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
379	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _
380	
381	@[simp]
382	lemma restrictFunctorId_hom_app_app :
383	    (restrictFunctorId.hom.app M).app U =
384	      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show U = 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U by simp)).op := rfl
385	
386	@[simp]
387	lemma restrictFunctorId_inv_app_app :
388	    (restrictFunctorId.inv.app M).app U =
389	      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U = U by simp)).op := rfl
390	
391	/-- Restriction along the composition is isomorphic to the composition of restrictions. -/
392	def restrictFunctorComp : restrictFunctor (f ≫ g) ≅ restrictFunctor g ⋙ restrictFunctor f :=
393	  have : (f.opensFunctor ⋙ g.opensFunctor).IsContinuous
394	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Z) :=
395	    Functor.isContinuous_comp _ _ _ (Opens.grothendieckTopology _) _
396	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp)) ≪≫
397	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
398	    (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _).symm
399	
400	@[simp]
401	lemma restrictFunctorComp_hom_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
402	    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl
403	
404	@[simp]
405	lemma restrictFunctorComp_inv_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
406	    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl
407	
408	/-- Restriction along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
409	def restrictFunctorCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g] :
410	    restrictFunctor f ≅ restrictFunctor g :=
411	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp [hf])) ≪≫
412	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; subst hf; simp)
413	
414	@[simp]
415	lemma restrictFunctorCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]
416	    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
417	    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl
418	
419	@[simp]
420	lemma restrictFunctorCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]
421	    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
422	    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl
423	
424	/-- Restriction along open immersions commutes with taking stalks. -/
425	def restrictStalkNatIso (x : X) :
426	    restrictFunctor f ⋙ toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ x ≅
427	    toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ (f x) :=
428	  haveI := Functor.initial_of_adjunction (f.isOpenEmbedding.adjunctionNhds x)
429	  (toPresheaf _ ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringLeft (OpenNhds (f x))ᵒᵖ Y.Opensᵒᵖ Ab).obj
430	      (OpenNhds.inclusion (f x)).op).isoWhiskerLeft
431	      (Functor.Final.colimIso (f.isOpenEmbedding.functorNhds x).op)
432	
433	@[simp]
434	lemma germ_restrictStalkNatIso_hom_app (x : X) (M : Y.Modules) (hxU : x ∈ U) :
435	    ((restrictFunctor f).obj M).presheaf.germ U _ hxU ≫
436	      (restrictStalkNatIso f x).hom.app M = M.presheaf.germ _ _ (by simpa) :=
437	  haveI := Functor.initial_of_adjunction (f.isOpenEmbedding.adjunctionNhds x)
438	  Functor.Final.ι_colimitIso_hom
439	    (f.isOpenEmbedding.functorNhds x).op
440	    ((OpenNhds.inclusion ((ConcreteCategory.hom f.base) x)).op ⋙ M.presheaf) _
441	
442	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
443	@[simp]
444	lemma germ_restrictStalkNatIso_inv_app (x : X) (M : Y.Modules) (hxU : x ∈ U) :
445	    M.presheaf.germ _ _ (by simpa) ≫ (restrictStalkNatIso f x).inv.app M =
446	      ((restrictFunctor f).obj M).presheaf.germ U _ hxU := by
447	  rw [← germ_restrictStalkNatIso_hom_app f x M hxU, Category.assoc, ← NatTrans.comp_app,
448	    Iso.hom_inv_id]
449	  simp
450	
451	end Restriction
452	
453	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

6	module

8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Closed.FunctorCategory.Basic

10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Equivalence

13	/-!
14	# Monoidal category structure on categories of sheaves
15	
16	If `A` is a closed braided category with suitable limits,
17	and `J` is a Grothendieck topology with `HasWeakSheafify J A`,
18	then `Sheaf J A` can be equipped with a monoidal category
19	structure. This is not made an instance as in some cases
20	it may conflict with monoidal structure deduced from
21	chosen finite products.
22	
23	## TODO
24	
25	* show that the monoidal category structure on sheaves is closed,
26	  and that the internal hom can be defined in such a way that the
27	  underlying presheaf is the internal hom in the category of presheaves.
28	  Note that a `MonoidalClosed` instance on sheaves can already be obtained
29	  abstractly using the material in `CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Braided.Reflection`.
30	
31	-/
32	
33	@[expose] public section
34	
35	universe v₁ v₂ v₃ u₁ u₂ u₃
36	
37	namespace CategoryTheory
38	
39	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
40	  {A : Type u₃} [Category.{v₃} A] [MonoidalCategory A]
41	
42	open Opposite Limits MonoidalCategory MonoidalClosed Enriched.FunctorCategory
43	
44	namespace Presheaf
45	
46	variable [MonoidalClosed A]
47	
48	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
49	/-- Relation between `functorEnrichedHom` and `presheafHom`. -/
50	noncomputable def functorEnrichedHomCoyonedaObjEquiv (M : A) (F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A)
51	    [HasFunctorEnrichedHom A F G] (X : C) :
52	    (functorEnrichedHom A F G ⋙ coyoneda.obj (op M)).obj (op X) ≃
53	    (presheafHom (F ⊗ (Functor.const _).obj M) G).obj (op X) where
54	  toFun f :=
55	    { app j := MonoidalClosed.uncurry (f ≫ enrichedHomπ A _ _ (Under.mk j.unop.hom.op))
56	      naturality j j' φ := by
57	        dsimp
58	        rw [tensorHom_id, ← uncurry_natural_right, ← uncurry_pre_app, Category.assoc,
59	          Category.assoc, ← enrichedOrdinaryCategorySelf_eHomWhiskerRight,
60	          ← enrichedOrdinaryCategorySelf_eHomWhiskerLeft]
61	        congr 2
62	        exact (enrichedHom_condition A (Under.forget (op X) ⋙ F) (Under.forget (op X) ⋙ G)
63	          (i := Under.mk j.unop.hom.op) (j := Under.mk j'.unop.hom.op)
64	            (Under.homMk φ.unop.left.op (Quiver.Hom.unop_inj (by simp)))).symm }
65	  invFun g :=
66	    end_.lift (fun j ↦ MonoidalClosed.curry (g.app (op (Over.mk j.hom.unop)))) (fun j j' φ ↦ by
67	      dsimp
68	      rw [enrichedOrdinaryCategorySelf_eHomWhiskerRight,
69	        enrichedOrdinaryCategorySelf_eHomWhiskerLeft,
70	        curry_pre_app, ← curry_natural_right]
71	      congr 1
72	      let α : Over.mk j'.hom.unop ⟶ Over.mk j.hom.unop := Over.homMk φ.right.unop
73	        (Quiver.Hom.op_inj (by simp))
74	      simpa using (g.naturality α.op).symm)
75	  left_inv f := by
76	    dsimp
77	    ext j
78	    dsimp
79	    simp only [curry_uncurry, end_.lift_π]
80	    rfl
81	  right_inv g := by
82	    dsimp
83	    ext j
84	    dsimp
85	    simp only [uncurry_curry, end_.lift_π]
86	    rfl
87	
88	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
89	lemma functorEnrichedHomCoyonedaObjEquiv_naturality
90	    {M : A} {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} {X Y : C} (f : X ⟶ Y)
91	    [HasFunctorEnrichedHom A F G]
92	    (y : (functorEnrichedHom A F G ⋙ coyoneda.obj (op M)).obj (op Y)) :
93	    functorEnrichedHomCoyonedaObjEquiv M F G X
94	      (y ≫ precompEnrichedHom' _ (Under.map f.op) (Iso.refl _) (Iso.refl _)) =
95	    (presheafHom (F ⊗ (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj M) G).map f.op
96	      (functorEnrichedHomCoyonedaObjEquiv M F G Y y) := by
97	  dsimp
98	  ext ⟨j⟩
99	  simp [functorEnrichedHomCoyonedaObjEquiv, presheafHom]
100	  rfl
101	
102	lemma isSheaf_functorEnrichedHom (F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) (hG : Presheaf.IsSheaf J G)
103	    [HasFunctorEnrichedHom A F G] :
104	    Presheaf.IsSheaf J (functorEnrichedHom A F G) := fun M ↦ by
105	  rw [Presieve.isSheaf_iff_of_nat_equiv
106	    (functorEnrichedHomCoyonedaObjEquiv M F G)
107	    (fun _ _ _ _ ↦ functorEnrichedHomCoyonedaObjEquiv_naturality _ _)]
108	  rw [← isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_of_type]
109	  exact Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom (F ⊗ (Functor.const _).obj M) G hG
110	
111	end Presheaf
112	
113	namespace GrothendieckTopology
114	
115	namespace W
116	
117	variable (J A) in
118	lemma transport_isMonoidal {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D] (K : GrothendieckTopology D)
119	    (G : D ⥤ C) [G.IsCoverDense J] [G.Full] [G.IsContinuous K J]
120	    [(G.sheafPushforwardContinuous A K J).EssSurj] [(K.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal] :
121	    (J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal := by
122	  rw [← J.W_inverseImage_whiskeringLeft K G]
123	  infer_instance
124	
125	variable [MonoidalClosed A]
126	  [∀ (F₁ F₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A), HasFunctorEnrichedHom A F₁ F₂]
127	  [∀ (F₁ F₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A), HasEnrichedHom A F₁ F₂]
128	
129	open MonoidalClosed.FunctorCategory
130	
131	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
132	lemma whiskerLeft {G₁ G₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} {g : G₁ ⟶ G₂} (hg : J.W g) (F : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) :
133	    J.W (F ◁ g) := fun H h ↦ by
134	  have := hg _ (Presheaf.isSheaf_functorEnrichedHom F H h)
135	  rw [← Function.Bijective.of_comp_iff' (f := MonoidalClosed.curry)
136	    ((ihom.adjunction _).homEquiv _ _).bijective]
137	  rw [← Function.Bijective.of_comp_iff (g := MonoidalClosed.curry) _
138	    ((ihom.adjunction _).homEquiv _ _).bijective] at this
139	  convert this using 1
140	  ext α : 1
141	  dsimp
142	  rw [curry_natural_left]
143	
144	lemma whiskerRight [BraidedCategory A]
145	    {F₁ F₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} {f : F₁ ⟶ F₂} (hf : J.W f) (G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) :
146	    J.W (f ▷ G) :=
147	  (J.W.arrow_mk_iso_iff (Arrow.isoMk (β_ F₁ G) (β_ F₂ G))).2 (hf.whiskerLeft G)
148	
149	instance monoidal [BraidedCategory A] : (J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal where
150	  whiskerLeft F _ _ _ hg := hg.whiskerLeft F
151	  whiskerRight _ hf G := hf.whiskerRight G
152	
153	end W
154	
155	end GrothendieckTopology
156	
157	namespace Sheaf
158	
159	variable (J A)
160	
161	/-- The monoidal category structure on `Sheaf J A` that is obtained
162	by localization of the monoidal category structure on the category
163	of presheaves. -/
164	@[instance_reducible]
165	noncomputable def monoidalCategory [(J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal] [HasWeakSheafify J A] :
166	    MonoidalCategory (Sheaf J A) :=
167	  inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategory
168	    (LocalizedMonoidal (L := presheafToSheaf J A) (W := J.W) (Iso.refl _)))
169	
170	attribute [local instance] monoidalCategory
171	
172	/-- The monoidal category structure on `Sheaf J A` obtained in `Sheaf.monoidalCategory` is
173	braided when `A` is braided. -/
174	@[implicit_reducible]
175	noncomputable def braidedCategory [(J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal] [HasWeakSheafify J A]
176	    [BraidedCategory A] : BraidedCategory (Sheaf J A) :=
177	  inferInstanceAs (BraidedCategory
178	    (LocalizedMonoidal (L := presheafToSheaf J A) (W := J.W) (Iso.refl _)))
179	
180	/-- The monoidal category structure on `Sheaf J A` obtained in `Sheaf.monoidalCategory` is
181	symmetric when `A` is symmetric. -/
182	@[implicit_reducible]
183	noncomputable def symmetricCategory [(J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal] [HasWeakSheafify J A]
184	    [SymmetricCategory A] :
185	    SymmetricCategory (Sheaf J A) :=
186	  inferInstanceAs (SymmetricCategory
187	    (LocalizedMonoidal (L := presheafToSheaf J A) (W := J.W) (Iso.refl _)))
188	
189	noncomputable instance [(J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal] [HasWeakSheafify J A] :
190	    (presheafToSheaf J A).Monoidal :=
191	  inferInstanceAs (Localization.Monoidal.toMonoidalCategory
192	    (L := presheafToSheaf J A) (W := J.W) (Iso.refl _)).Monoidal
193	
194	noncomputable instance [(J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal] [HasWeakSheafify J A] [BraidedCategory A] :
195	    letI := braidedCategory J A
196	    (presheafToSheaf J A).Braided :=
197	  inferInstanceAs (Localization.Monoidal.toMonoidalCategory
198	    (L := presheafToSheaf J A) (W := J.W) (Iso.refl _)).Braided
199	
200	noncomputable example
201	    [HasWeakSheafify J A] [MonoidalClosed A] [BraidedCategory A]
202	    [∀ (F₁ F₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A), HasFunctorEnrichedHom A F₁ F₂]
203	    [∀ (F₁ F₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A), HasEnrichedHom A F₁ F₂] :
204	    MonoidalCategory (Sheaf J A) :=
205	  monoidalCategory J A
206	
207	noncomputable example
208	    [HasWeakSheafify J A] [MonoidalClosed A] [BraidedCategory A]
209	    [∀ (F₁ F₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A), HasFunctorEnrichedHom A F₁ F₂]
210	    [∀ (F₁ F₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A), HasEnrichedHom A F₁ F₂] :
211	    BraidedCategory (Sheaf J A) :=
212	  braidedCategory J A
213	
214	noncomputable example
215	    [HasWeakSheafify J A] [MonoidalClosed A] [SymmetricCategory A]
216	    [∀ (F₁ F₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A), HasFunctorEnrichedHom A F₁ F₂]
217	    [∀ (F₁ F₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A), HasEnrichedHom A F₁ F₂] :
218	    SymmetricCategory (Sheaf J A) :=
219	  symmetricCategory J A
220	
221	end Sheaf
